title: Allowing Both Safe and Unsafe HTTP Methods: Security Sensitive
description: An HTTP method is safe when used to perform a read-only operation, such as retrieving information. Allowing both safe and unsafe HTTP methods to perform a specific operation on a web application could impact its security.
recommendation: Use only safe HTTP methods for read-only operations and only unsafe HTTP methods for state-changing operations.
impact: Impact on security due to potential CSRF vulnerabilities and other security risks.

checks.message: Allowing both safe and unsafe HTTP methods could impact the security of the web application.
The method should only be a safe HTTP method for read-only operations.
